footer.tagline: Helping students discover Romania and navigate every step of studying abroad.

home.heroDesc: We guide students through every step -- from choosing a university to landing in Romania. EU-recognised degrees, affordable tuition, and a continent to explore.
home.whyDesc: A hidden gem in the heart of Europe -- world-class education at a fraction of the cost.
home.servicesDesc: Complete services from your first inquiry to your first day of class.
home.universitiesDesc: From the oldest institutions in south-eastern Europe to the newest research powerhouses.
home.ctaDesc: Book a free consultation today and let us map your path to your dream university.

whyRomania.title: More than Dracula's castle
whyRomania.desc: Medieval cities, cutting-edge research, affordable living and a continent at your doorstep. Romania is Europe's best-kept secret for international students.
whyRomania.ctaDesc: Book a free consultation and take the first step toward studying in Romania.

01.title: EU-Recognised Degrees
01.body: Romanian universities are part of the Bologna Process, meaning your degree and Diploma Supplement are automatically recognised across all EU member states and beyond. Whether you pursue a career in Europe, Canada, or the Gulf, your qualification opens doors.

02.title: Affordable Tuition & Living
02.body: Romania has some of the EU's lowest tuition fees and cost of living. A student can comfortably cover accommodation, food, transport and leisure on €400-600/month in most cities -- a fraction of Western European costs with none of the compromise on quality.

03.title: Study in Multiple Languages
03.body: With over 100 accredited institutions, you can pursue programmes in English, French, German, Romanian or Hungarian. This multilingual environment enriches your academic experience and opens future career paths across language markets.

04.title: World-Class Internet
04.body: Romania consistently ranks among the top 3 countries globally for internet connectivity and speed. For research, remote internships, or simply staying connected with family, Romania's digital infrastructure is second to none.

05.title: Rich History & Culture
05.body: Home to 7 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, medieval cities, painted monasteries, Carpathian mountains and Black Sea beaches -- Romania is endlessly explorable. With 210 sunny days per year and a vibrant student cultural scene, life here is full.

06.title: Safety & Quality of Life
06.body: Romania is consistently ranked among the safest countries in Europe, with a crime rate well below the EU average. The population of ~20 million is warm and hospitable, and the quality of air ranks 48th worldwide by WHO standards.

07.title: Academic Tradition
07.body: Romania has a centuries-old academic heritage. The University of Iași (1860) and University of Bucharest (1864) are among the oldest in south-eastern Europe. Four Romanian universities feature in the QS World University Rankings.

08.title: Part-Time Work Opportunities
08.body: As an international student you can work part-time -- BPO centres, startups and NGOs actively recruit bilingual students. Student discounts on transport, culture and accommodation further reduce expenses.

universities.title: Romanian Universities
universities.desc: Browse top Romanian universities for international students. Full English, French and German-language programmes.
universities.ctaDesc: Our team's extensive experience across all Romanian universities will help you make the right choice.

universityOfBucharest.name: University of Bucharest
universityOfBucharest.highlight: Second oldest HEI in Romania. 18 faculties. Partners in 50 institutions across 40 countries. QS World ranked.
universityOfBucharest.fullDesc: The University of Bucharest was founded in 1864 by a decree of Prince Alexander Ioan Cuza, transforming the Academy of Saint Sava into the first national university. Today it has 18 faculties covering a wide range of disciplines and cooperates with more than 50 institutions of higher education in 40 countries through international exchange programmes.

babesBolyai.name: Babeș-Bolyai University
babesBolyai.highlight: Largest university in Romania. #1 national ranking. 21 colleges. Research-focused.
babesBolyai.fullDesc: Founded in 1919 (with roots to 1581), Babeș-Bolyai University is the largest higher education institution in Romania, ranked first by the Romanian Ministry of Education and Research. With 21 colleges offering undergraduate, master's and PhD programmes in multiple languages, it attracts students from across the world.

iuliuHatieganu.name: Iuliu Hațieganu University of Medicine & Pharmacy
iuliuHatieganu.highlight: Oldest medical school in Transylvania. 9,000+ students, 3,000 resident physicians.
iuliuHatieganu.fullDesc: The oldest medical education institution in Transylvania, Iuliu Hațieganu University traces its origins to a faculty of medicine founded in 1919 as part of the University of Upper Dacia. It now hosts over 9,000 local and international students, 3,000 resident physicians and more than 1,200 teachers and researchers.

alexandruCuza.name: Alexandru Ioan Cuza University
alexandruCuza.highlight: Oldest university in Romania. Founded by Prince Alexandru Ioan Cuza. QS ranked.
alexandruCuza.fullDesc: Founded in 1860 by Prince Alexandru Ioan Cuza, this is the oldest university in Romania. Located in Iași, north-eastern Romania, it is a comprehensive research university with strong humanities and sciences traditions.

westTimisoara.name: West University of Timișoara
westTimisoara.highlight: Ranked 4th among Romanian universities globally. Top 800 QS World Universities.
westTimisoara.fullDesc: Established in 1962 and ranked as the 4th Romanian university in global tables, the West University of Timișoara is a comprehensive institution noted for its multidisciplinary approach and strong international connections. Timișoara, the 2023 European Capital of Culture, provides a vibrant backdrop for student life.

polytehnicaTimisoara.name: Politehnica University of Timișoara
polytehnicaTimisoara.highlight: Engineering-focused. Civil, Chemical, Electronic, Mechanical and more.
polytehnicaTimisoara.fullDesc: Founded in 1920, Politehnica University of Timișoara is the leading engineering education institution in north-western Romania, offering 16 engineering disciplines and collaborating with major European companies in research and development.

cities.title: Student cities in Romania
cities.desc: From the grand capital to charming Transylvanian towns -- each city has its own personality and advantages.
cities.ctaTitle: Ready to move to your city?
cities.ctaDesc: Our local advisors in every city will help you settle in fast.

bucharest.name: Bucharest
bucharest.tagline: The capital -- grand, dynamic and full of opportunity
bucharest.body: Bucharest is Romania's political, economic and cultural capital. Known as the "Paris of the East" for its wide boulevards and Belle Époque architecture, it houses more than 40 museums and a thriving arts and startup scene. The city never sleeps -- world-class restaurants, massive music festivals and a growing tech industry make it an exciting base for ambitious students.

clujNapoca.name: Cluj-Napoca
clujNapoca.tagline: Romania's student capital -- young, energetic, tech-forward
clujNapoca.body: Often called the "Silicon Valley of Eastern Europe", Cluj-Napoca is Romania's fastest-growing city and the undisputed student capital. Home to Babeș-Bolyai University (the largest in Romania), it hosts Electric Castle, one of Europe's leading music festivals. The city blends medieval old town charm with a modern startup ecosystem and an incredibly international student community.

timisoara.name: Timișoara
timisoara.tagline: 2023 European Capital of Culture -- multicultural and welcoming
timisoara.body: Timișoara, the 2023 European Capital of Culture, is a truly multicultural city near the borders of Hungary and Serbia. Its Baroque city centre is one of the most beautiful in Romania and the city's café culture, street art and international connections make it a fantastic place to study. It's also one of the most affordable major cities for students.

iasi.name: Iași
iasi.tagline: The cultural capital of Moldova -- historic and affordable
iasi.body: Iași (pronounced "Yash") is Romania's cultural and academic heart in the north-east. Home to the oldest university in the country (Alexandru Ioan Cuza, 1860), it's steeped in history with stunning Neogothic architecture, beautiful parks and an intimate student atmosphere. It's also the most affordable major university city in Romania.

targuMures.name: Târgu Mureș
targuMures.body: Târgu Mureș is a compact, bilingual (Romanian-Hungarian) Transylvanian city that punches above its weight in medical education. Home to the UMFST university, it attracts international medical students from across the globe. The city is safe, friendly and far more affordable than larger cities, making it ideal for focused study.

life.title: Life in Romania
life.desc: Everything you need to know about student life in Romania -- climate, culture, food, costs and more.
life.ctaDesc: Our on-the-ground team in Bucharest, Cluj-Napoca and Timișoara is ready to receive you.

welcome.title: Welcome to Romania
welcome.content:
Romania sits at the crossroads of Central, Eastern and South-Eastern Europe, bordering Hungary, Serbia, Bulgaria, Ukraine, Republic of Moldova and the Black Sea. With an area of 238,391 km² -- 12th largest in Europe -- and a population of approximately 20 million, it is the 9th largest EU country by area. Bucharest, the capital, is home to over 2 million people.

The word "Romania" first came into use when three historic regions -- Wallachia, Moldavia and Transylvania -- were unified in 1859, reflecting the country's Latin heritage rooted in ancient Rome. Today its landscape is perfectly balanced between mountains (31%), hills (33%) and plains (36%).

climate.title: Climate & Seasons
climate.content:
Romania has a temperate-continental climate with four distinct seasons and around 210 sunny days per year.

Spring is delightful -- cool mornings warming to 20°C afternoons. Summer is hot and dry in the south (up to 35°C in July-August), more comfortable in the mountains. Autumn is dry with colourful foliage -- September especially is ideal for visiting. Winter runs December-February, with average temperatures of −3°C in Bucharest; snowfall is common in the plains and heavy in the mountains.

Annual average temperature is 11°C in the south and 8°C in the north-east.

culture.title: Culture & People
culture.content:
Romania is ethnically and culturally diverse: Romanian (88.9%), Hungarian (6.6%), Roma (3.3%), Ukrainian, German and Turkish minorities. The official language is Romanian -- a Latin language with about 1,700 years of history. Hungarian is widely spoken in Transylvania; German in Sibiu and Brașov areas.

The dominant religion is Orthodox Christianity, with significant Catholic, Baptist, Pentecostal and Muslim minorities. Romanians are known for their warmth and hospitality -- guests are always offered food and drink, and greetings among friends typically involve a kiss on both cheeks.

Romania has a vibrant folk tradition: painted eggs at Easter, hand-crafted pottery, textile embroidery, wood carving, folk masks and traditional costumes worn at festivals.

food.title: Food in Romania
food.content:
Romanian cuisine is hearty and flavourful, built around seasonal ingredients and slow-cooked dishes.

Typical breakfasts are savoury -- eggs, sandwiches with butter and cold cuts, or pastries with jam, usually with coffee or tea. The main meal is at midday and typically includes a soup or ciorbă (sour soup) followed by a meat-based main course. Popular dishes include:

- Mititei: grilled minced-meat rolls with garlic, a Romanian staple
- Sarmale: cabbage or vine leaves stuffed with spiced rice and meat
- Mămăligă: polenta served with sour cream and eggs
- Tocană: slow-cooked pork with garlic and onion
- Borș de miel: lamb and vegetable sour soup

Romania produces excellent wines (mostly red from Dealu Mare) and the local plum brandy țuică is a national tradition.

costs.title: Cost of Living
costs.content:
Romania offers outstanding value for international students -- one of the lowest costs of living in the EU while maintaining a high quality of life.

- Dormitory room: €80-150/mo
- Private room / studio: €180-350/mo
- Monthly groceries: €100-150
- Public transport pass: €10-20
- Mobile phone plan: €5-10
- Eating out (lunch): €4-8
- Total estimate: €400-650/month

Students receive significant discounts on public transport, museum entry and cultural events. Part-time work opportunities exist at call centres, startups and student organisations.

academics.title: Academics & University Life
academics.content:
Romanian universities participate in the Bologna Process for unified European higher education, ensuring mutual recognition of qualifications. Most foreign-language programmes operate to international standards while integrating students into the local community.

The university year typically runs September/October to January (Semester 1) and February to June (Semester 2). The academic year ends with exams in July. Student life is active with academic clubs, sports organisations and cultural associations.

services.title: Complete student services
services.desc: From your first inquiry to your first day of class, we handle every detail so you can focus on your future.
services.ctaDesc: Book a free consultation and let us map your route to studying in Romania.

translation.title: Document Translation & Attestation
translation.desc: We handle the certified translation of all your academic documents (diplomas, transcripts, certificates) into Romanian or other required languages, plus official attestation stamps from the relevant authorities.

consular.title: Consular & Notarial Confirmations
consular.desc: We coordinate consular attestations and notarial confirmations of your documents at Romanian embassies or in your home country so your credentials meet university requirements.

admission.title: University Application
admission.desc: From choosing the right program and institution to preparing your application file and submitting it to the relevant admissions office -- we manage the entire process.

visa.title: Student Visa Assistance
visa.desc: We guide you through Romanian student visa requirements, help you compile your visa dossier, and liaise with the consulate to minimise delays.

travel.title: Travel & Airport Reception
travel.desc: We assist with booking your flight and ensure a smooth arrival -- our team receives you at the airport and safely transfers you to your accommodation on day one.

accommodation.title: Accommodation Booking
accommodation.desc: We arrange university dormitory placements or vetted private apartments so you have a comfortable, safe home ready before you land.

residency.title: Residency, Insurance & Banking
residency.desc: Once you arrive, we assist with registering your residence permit, enrolling in health insurance, and opening a Romanian bank account -- the three essentials of student life.

support.title: Ongoing Student Support
support.desc: Our support doesn't end at arrival. We stay available throughout your studies to help with administrative issues, academic navigation, and anything else that comes up.

pricing.title: Transparent pricing
pricing.desc: No hidden fees. Choose the plan that fits your needs.
pricing.ctaDesc: Book a free 15-minute call with one of our advisors to help you choose the right plan.

essential.name: Essential
essential.description: For students who just need the paperwork handled.

fullService.name: Full Service
fullService.description: Everything covered -- from your first document to your front door.

complete.name: Complete
complete.description: The full end-to-end experience -- we don't stop until you're settled.

faqs.a: A 50% deposit is required to start the process. The remaining 50% is due once you receive your university admission letter.

faqs.q: What if my application is rejected?
faqs.a: If your application is rejected due to our error, we will resubmit at no extra charge. If required documents are missing on your side, we will advise you on the fastest remediation path.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade my plan later?
faqs.a: Yes. You can upgrade from Essential to Full Service or Complete at any time by paying the difference.

faqs.q: Do you work with students from all countries?
faqs.a: Yes, we work with students from all over the world. We specialise in serving students from Arab countries, Africa and Asia, but we welcome students from everywhere.

about.title: Driven by experience, committed to your success
about.desc: EduRomania was born from a real experience of studying in Romania as a foreign student. We're here because no one was here for us.
about.ourStoryBody: Our founder, Ziad Al-Otleh, arrived in Romania as an international student and navigated the bureaucracy, document marathons and language barriers that so many face. EduRomania was born from that experience -- from the desire to ensure every student can focus on their studies and experiences, not paperwork and administrative hurdles.
about.ctaTitle: Ready to join thousands of graduates?
about.ctaDesc: Book a free consultation today and let's get started together.

mission.title: Mission
mission.body: To remove every barrier between a motivated student and a high-quality Romanian university education. We believe geography and bureaucracy should never stop talent.

approach.title: Approach
approach.body: We treat each student as an individual. Our advisors take time to understand your goals, budget and aspirations before recommending any path.

experience.title: Experience
experience.body: Our team has guided hundreds of international students to Romanian universities. We know every institution, every city and every administrative nuance.

commitment.title: Commitment
commitment.body: We don't disappear after your visa is stamped. Our relationship continues throughout your studies and into your early career.

founder.name: Ziad Al-Otleh
founder.bio: Having navigated the Romanian university system firsthand as an international student, Ziad founded EduRomania to ensure no student has to face the process alone. Fluent in Arabic, English and Romanian.

advisor.name: Student Advisor
advisor.bio: Our admissions specialist has in-depth knowledge of every Romanian university's requirements and maintains direct relationships with admissions offices across the country.

local.name: Local Support
local.bio: Based in Bucharest, Cluj-Napoca and Timișoara, our local team ensures students are received, settled and supported from the moment they land.

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.desc: Have questions about studying in Romania? Ready to start your application? Book a free consultation or send us a message -- we reply within 24 hours.
